description: Full project backlog. Stories are pulled into increment sprint files by ceremonies/plan.md.
philosophy: Every increment leaves the repo in a state where something real can be verified. No story is complete without a passing behavioral test. Build little by little, always on green.

stories.title: Plan ceremony: always queue a pending increment stub when creating an active sprint
stories.description: GGE G5 fires every sprint because the plan ceremony creates an active increment but never queues a follow-on pending increment. The fix: at the end of the plan ceremony, if no increment has status='pending' in manifest.json, automatically append a minimal pending increment stub (id, name, description, status='pending', dependsOn current). This ensures GGE G5 never fires again and the planning pipeline is always primed.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/plan.md (or the plan ceremony runner) contains logic to append a pending increment stub if none exists

After running plan ceremony on a fresh sprint, python3 -c "import json; m=json.load(open('prd/manifest.json')); pending=[i for i in m.get('increments',[]) if i.get('status')=='pending']; assert len(pending)>=1; print('OK')" exits 0
shellcheck -S error scripts/ralph/ceremonies/*.sh exits 0 (if any .sh files are modified)
stories.returnedReason: Root cause of recurring GGE-G5 ANDON: plan ceremony does not queue a pending increment after creating an active sprint, so G5 fires every sprint cycle.
stories.readinessNote: NOT READY: (1) testPlan field is missing — add specific commands to verify plan ceremony appends a pending stub when none exists. (2) AC1 is not independently verifiable ('contains logic' is not a command) — rewrite as: "grep -n 'pending\|stub' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/plan.md returns a match". Fix both before pulling into a sprint.

stories.title: Kaizen: ArgoCD root-app.yaml hardcodes github.com repoURL — migrate to internal GitLab after platform bootstrap
stories.description: platform/argocd-apps/root-app.yaml hardcodes repoURL: https://github.com/libliflin/sovereign. Once GitLab (PLATFORM-005) is deployed and operational, ArgoCD should point to the internal GitLab instance rather than the external GitHub URL. Until this migration is made, the platform's GitOps engine depends on an external service — violating the T1 Sovereignty invariant. This was identified during constitution-review increment-35 by noting root-app.yaml was the only ArgoCD app without global.domain and that it references an external host.

stories.title: Kaizen: SMART-score all 16 unscored open backlog stories to clear planning WARN

stories.title: SMART guidance: ACs asserting vendor API field values must cite chart version CRD spec
stories.description: TEST-004b failed review three times in sprint 37 because AC3 asserted 'phase=Finished' — the documented chaos-mesh phase — but v2.6.3 uses 'Not Injected' as the terminal recovery state. The implementation was correct; the AC was wrong. Root cause: no SMART gate requires that ACs asserting specific API field values (phase names, condition strings, status keys) be cross-referenced against the pinned chart version's CRD spec or empirically verified before the story is written. Add an explicit rule to scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md: when an AC asserts a vendor-specific status field value (e.g. 'phase=X', 'condition=Y'), the story must either cite the upstream CRD documentation for the pinned chart version or note that the value was empirically confirmed against a running instance.

stories.title: Kaizen: Audit pinned Helm dependency versions — identify charts with dependencies >12 months behind upstream
stories.description: The platform has 15+ charts with pinned upstream Helm dependency versions (ArgoCD 6.7.3, Istio 1.21.2, Crossplane 1.17.0, Harbor 1.15.0, prometheus-stack 58.2.2, etc.). No gate or process checks whether these drift stale. Stale chart versions accumulate: (1) unpatched CVEs in vendor images, (2) deprecated API versions that must be migrated before the chart can be upgraded, (3) incompatibilities between components that compound the longer upgrade is deferred. KAIZEN-003 already handles the Istio v1beta1→v1 API migration before the version bump. This story performs the full audit: for each chart with a pinned dependency, record the current pinned version and the latest stable release, flag any chart more than one major version or 12 months behind, and produce a prioritized upgrade list in docs/state/dependency-versions.md. No upgrades are performed — audit only.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/state/dependency-versions.md exists and lists every chart in platform/charts/ that has a dependencies entry in Chart.yaml
Each entry in docs/state/dependency-versions.md includes: chart name, current pinned version, latest stable version (from upstream Helm repo), and a staleness flag (current/stale/critical)
grep -c 'stale\|critical' docs/state/dependency-versions.md prints a number (0 is acceptable if all dependencies are current; the file must exist regardless)
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py exits 0
stories.testPlan: cat docs/state/dependency-versions.md — must show all charts with pinned deps. grep the file for each chart name from platform/charts/*/Chart.yaml that has a dependencies: section. Show verbatim output.
